name: LangGraph
summary: Library for building stateful, multi-actor agent workflows as graphs with cycles, persistence, and human-in-the-loop patterns.
strengths: Explicit graph model suits complex control flow and retries
Integrates with LangChain ecosystem and common LLM providers
Supports durable execution patterns useful for production agents
weaknesses: Conceptual overhead versus linear chains for simple tasks
Operational behavior depends on deployment and checkpointing choices
best_for: Multi-step agents with branching, loops, or approval gates
Teams already invested in Python and LangChain-style tooling
avoid_for: Trivial single-call LLM wrappers where a thin SDK suffices
